entries.name: member
entries.summary: Return the tail of the list starting from the first matching element.
entries.body:
Return the tail of the list starting from the first matching element.

```sema
(member 3 '(1 2 3 4))   ; => (3 4)
(member 9 '(1 2 3))     ; => #f
```

entries.name: nth
entries.summary: Return the element at index N (zero-based).
entries.body:
Return the element at index N (zero-based).

```sema
(nth '(10 20 30) 1)   ; => 20
(nth '(10 20 30) 0)   ; => 10
```

entries.name: partition
entries.summary: Split a list into two lists based on a predicate. Returns a list of two lists: elements that satisfy the predicate and those that don't.
entries.body:
Split a list into two lists based on a predicate. Returns a list of two lists: elements that satisfy the predicate and those that don't.

```sema
(partition even? '(1 2 3 4 5))   ; => ((2 4) (1 3 5))
```

entries.name: range
entries.summary: Generate a list of integers. With one argument, generates 0 to N-1. With two, generates from start to end-1.
entries.body:
Generate a list of integers. With one argument, generates 0 to N-1. With two, generates from start to end-1.

```sema
(range 5)       ; => (0 1 2 3 4)
(range 1 5)     ; => (1 2 3 4)
```
entries.syntax: (range end) | (range start end) | (range start end step)

entries.name: reduce
entries.summary: Like `foldl` but uses the first element as the initial value.
entries.body:
Like `foldl` but uses the first element as the initial value.

```sema
(reduce + '(1 2 3 4 5))   ; => 15
```

entries.name: rest
entries.summary: Alias for `cdr`. Return the rest of the list.
entries.body:
Alias for `cdr`. Return the rest of the list.

```sema
(rest '(1 2 3))    ; => (2 3)
```

entries.name: reverse
entries.summary: Reverse a list.
entries.body:
Reverse a list.

```sema
(reverse '(1 2 3))   ; => (3 2 1)
```

entries.name: some?
entries.summary: Return `#t` if `pred` returns truthy for at least one element of `seq`. Alias of `any` (and of `any?`).
entries.body:
Return `#t` if `pred` returns truthy for at least one element of `seq`. Alias of `any` (and of `any?`).

```sema
(some? even? '(1 3 4))   ; => #t
(some? even? '(1 3 5))   ; => #f
```

entries.name: sort
entries.summary: Sort a list in ascending order.
entries.body:
Sort a list in ascending order.

```sema
(sort '(3 1 4 1 5))   ; => (1 1 3 4 5)
```

entries.name: sort-by
entries.summary: Sort a list by a key function.
entries.body:
Sort a list by a key function.

```sema
(sort-by length '("bb" "a" "ccc"))   ; => ("a" "bb" "ccc")
(sort-by abs '(-3 1 -2))             ; => (1 -2 -3)
```

entries.name: take
entries.summary: Take the first N elements.
entries.body:
Take the first N elements.

```sema
(take 3 '(1 2 3 4 5))   ; => (1 2 3)
(take 10 '(1 2))         ; => (1 2)
```

entries.name: take-while
entries.summary: Return the leading elements of `seq` for which `pred` is truthy, stopping at the first element that fails. Same as `list/take-while`.
entries.body:
Return the leading elements of `seq` for which `pred` is truthy, stopping at the first element that fails. Same as `list/take-while`.

```sema
(take-while (fn (x) (< x 4)) '(1 2 3 4 1))   ; => (1 2 3)
```

entries.name: tap
entries.summary: Apply a side-effect function to a value, then return the original value.
entries.body:
Apply a side-effect function to a value, then return the original value.

```sema
(tap 42 (fn (x) (println x)))   ; prints 42, returns 42
```

entries.name: zip
entries.summary: Combine corresponding elements from two lists into pairs.
entries.body:
Combine corresponding elements from two lists into pairs.

```sema
(zip '(1 2 3) '("a" "b" "c"))   ; => ((1 "a") (2 "b") (3 "c"))
```

entries.name: llm/auto-configure
entries.summary: Auto-configure providers from environment variables (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `GROQ_API_KEY`, `XAI_API_KEY`, `MISTRAL_API_KEY`, `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`, `GOOGLE_API_KEY`, plus embedding keys like `JINA_API_KEY`, `VOYAGE_API_KEY`, `COHERE_API_KEY`). Ollama is always registered as a local fallback. Honors `SEMA_CHAT_PROVIDER`/`SEMA_CHAT_MODEL` and `SEMA_EMBEDDING_PROVIDER`/`SEMA_EMBEDDING_MODEL` overrides. Returns the default provider keyword, or nil if none configured.
entries.body:
Auto-configure providers from environment variables (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `GROQ_API_KEY`, `XAI_API_KEY`, `MISTRAL_API_KEY`, `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`, `GOOGLE_API_KEY`, plus embedding keys like `JINA_API_KEY`, `VOYAGE_API_KEY`, `COHERE_API_KEY`). Ollama is always registered as a local fallback. Honors `SEMA_CHAT_PROVIDER`/`SEMA_CHAT_MODEL` and `SEMA_EMBEDDING_PROVIDER`/`SEMA_EMBEDDING_MODEL` overrides. Returns the default provider keyword, or nil if none configured.

```sema
(llm/auto-configure)   ; => :anthropic  (if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set)
```

entries.name: llm/batch
entries.summary: Send a list of prompt strings to the default provider in parallel (via the provider's batch completion) and return a list of completion strings in the same order. The opts map accepts `:model`, `:max-tokens`, `:temperature`, and `:system`.
entries.body:
Send a list of prompt strings to the default provider in parallel (via the provider's batch completion) and return a list of completion strings in the same order. The opts map accepts `:model`, `:max-tokens`, `:temperature`, and `:system`.

```sema
(llm/batch ["Capital of France?" "Capital of Japan?"] {:max-tokens 20})
```

entries.name: llm/budget-remaining
entries.summary: Return the current budget status, or nil if no budget is set. When a cost budget is active the map includes `:limit`, `:spent`, and `:remaining` (USD); when a token budget is active it includes `:token-limit`, `:tokens-spent`, and `:tokens-remaining`.
entries.body:
Return the current budget status, or nil if no budget is set. When a cost budget is active the map includes `:limit`, `:spent`, and `:remaining` (USD); when a token budget is active it includes `:token-limit`, `:tokens-spent`, and `:tokens-remaining`.

```sema
(llm/set-budget 1.0)
(llm/budget-remaining)   ; => {:limit 1.0 :spent 0.0 :remaining 1.0}
```

entries.name: llm/cache-clear
entries.summary: Clear the LLM response cache: empties the in-memory cache, deletes cached `.json` files from the on-disk cache directory, and resets hit/miss counters. Returns the number of in-memory entries that were removed.
entries.body:
Clear the LLM response cache: empties the in-memory cache, deletes cached `.json` files from the on-disk cache directory, and resets hit/miss counters. Returns the number of in-memory entries that were removed.

```sema
(llm/cache-clear)   ; => 12
```

entries.name: llm/cache-key
entries.summary: Compute the cache key string that would be used for a given prompt and options. The opts map accepts `:model`, `:temperature`, and `:system`. Useful for inspecting or pre-seeding the response cache.
entries.body:
Compute the cache key string that would be used for a given prompt and options. The opts map accepts `:model`, `:temperature`, and `:system`. Useful for inspecting or pre-seeding the response cache.

```sema
(llm/cache-key "hello" {:model "gpt-4o-mini"})   ; => "a1b2c3..."
```

entries.name: llm/cache-stats
entries.summary: Return cache statistics as a map with `:hits`, `:misses`, and `:size` (the number of entries currently in the in-memory cache).
entries.body:
Return cache statistics as a map with `:hits`, `:misses`, and `:size` (the number of entries currently in the in-memory cache).

```sema
(llm/cache-stats)   ; => {:hits 3 :misses 1 :size 4}
```

entries.name: llm/cassette-eject
entries.summary: Flush the active cassette's tape to disk and remove it, so subsequent LLM calls hit the real provider again. Returns `#t` if a cassette was active, `#f` otherwise.
entries.body:
Flush the active cassette's tape to disk and remove it, so subsequent LLM calls hit the real provider again. Returns `#t` if a cassette was active, `#f` otherwise.

```sema
(llm/cassette-eject)
```

entries.name: llm/cassette-load
entries.summary: Install a cassette globally (record/replay of LLM calls) from a tape file. The opts map is optional and accepts `:mode` (`:auto` default, `:record`, or `:replay`). Use `llm/cassette-save` to flush and `llm/cassette-eject` to remove it. For scoped use prefer `llm/with-cassette`.
entries.body:
Install a cassette globally (record/replay of LLM calls) from a tape file. The opts map is optional and accepts `:mode` (`:auto` default, `:record`, or `:replay`). Use `llm/cassette-save` to flush and `llm/cassette-eject` to remove it. For scoped use prefer `llm/with-cassette`.

```sema
(llm/cassette-load "tapes/suite.jsonl" {:mode :replay})
```

entries.name: llm/cassette-save
entries.summary: Flush the active cassette's tape to disk. Returns `#t` if a cassette is active, `#f` otherwise. (The tape is also flushed automatically when an `llm/with-cassette` scope exits.)
entries.body:
Flush the active cassette's tape to disk. Returns `#t` if a cassette is active, `#f` otherwise. (The tape is also flushed automatically when an `llm/with-cassette` scope exits.)

```sema
(llm/cassette-save)
```

entries.name: llm/chat
entries.summary: Run a multi-message chat against the default provider and return the assistant's text reply. The first argument is a sequence of messages. The optional opts map accepts `:model`, `:max-tokens`, `:temperature`, `:system`, `:tools`, `:tool-mode` (`:auto` or `:none`), and `:max-tool-rounds` (default 10). When tools are supplied and tool-mode is not `:none`, it runs a tool-execution loop.
entries.body:
Run a multi-message chat against the default provider and return the assistant's text reply. The first argument is a sequence of messages. The optional opts map accepts `:model`, `:max-tokens`, `:temperature`, `:system`, `:tools`, `:tool-mode` (`:auto` or `:none`), and `:max-tool-rounds` (default 10). When tools are supplied and tool-mode is not `:none`, it runs a tool-execution loop.

```sema
(llm/chat (list (message :user "What is the capital of France?"))
          {:model "gpt-4o-mini"})
```

entries.name: llm/classify
entries.summary: Classify text into exactly one of the given categories. The first argument is a sequence of category names (keywords or strings); the model is told to respond with only the matching category name. Returns a keyword if the matched category was supplied as a keyword, otherwise a string. The opts map accepts `:model`.
entries.body:
Classify text into exactly one of the given categories. The first argument is a sequence of category names (keywords or strings); the model is told to respond with only the matching category name. Returns a keyword if the matched category was supplied as a keyword, otherwise a string. The opts map accepts `:model`.

```sema
(llm/classify [:positive :negative :neutral] "I absolutely love this product!")
; => :positive
```

entries.name: llm/clear-budget
entries.summary: Clear any active budget limit, removing both the cost and token budget caps.
entries.body:
Clear any active budget limit, removing both the cost and token budget caps.

```sema
(llm/clear-budget)
```

entries.name: llm/compare
entries.summary: Compare two texts using the default provider and return a parsed JSON map with `:similarity` (0.0–1.0), `:differences` (a list of key differences), and `:summary` (a brief comparison). The opts map accepts `:model`.
examples: (llm/compare "The cat sat on the mat" "A cat is sitting on a rug")
entries.body:
Compare two texts using the default provider and return a parsed JSON map with `:similarity` (0.0–1.0), `:differences` (a list of key differences), and `:summary` (a brief comparison). The opts map accepts `:model`.

```sema
(llm/compare "The cat sat on the mat" "A cat is sitting on a rug")
```

entries.name: llm/complete
entries.summary: Send a single prompt to the default provider and return the completion text. The first argument is a prompt string (or a prompt value). The optional opts map accepts `:model`, `:max-tokens` (defaults to 4096), `:temperature`, and `:system`. Tracks token usage.
entries.body:
Send a single prompt to the default provider and return the completion text. The first argument is a prompt string (or a prompt value). The optional opts map accepts `:model`, `:max-tokens` (defaults to 4096), `:temperature`, and `:system`. Tracks token usage.

```sema
(llm/complete "Write a haiku about autumn" {:max-tokens 100 :temperature 0.7})
```

entries.name: llm/configure
entries.summary: Configure and register an LLM provider. Chat providers (`:anthropic`, `:openai`, `:gemini`, `:groq`, `:xai`, `:mistral`, `:moonshot`, `:ollama`, or any unknown name treated as OpenAI-compatible) become the default chat provider. Embedding providers (`:jina`, `:voyage`, `:cohere`) are registered as the embedding provider instead — equivalent to `llm/configure-embeddings`. The map carries `:api-key`, optional `:default-model`, and `:base-url`/`:host` depending on the provider. Returns nil.
entries.body:
Configure and register an LLM provider. Chat providers (`:anthropic`, `:openai`, `:gemini`, `:groq`, `:xai`, `:mistral`, `:moonshot`, `:ollama`, or any unknown name treated as OpenAI-compatible) become the default chat provider. Embedding providers (`:jina`, `:voyage`, `:cohere`) are registered as the embedding provider instead — equivalent to `llm/configure-embeddings`. The map carries `:api-key`, optional `:default-model`, and `:base-url`/`:host` depending on the provider. Returns nil.

```sema
(llm/configure :anthropic {:api-key "sk-ant-..." :default-model "claude-sonnet-4-6"})
```

entries.name: llm/configure-embeddings
entries.summary: Configure and register the embedding provider used by `llm/embed`. The provider keyword may be `:jina`, `:voyage`, `:cohere`, or any other (treated as OpenAI-compatible). The map carries `:api-key`, optional `:default-model`/`:model`, and `:base-url` for OpenAI-compatible endpoints. Returns nil.
entries.body:
Configure and register the embedding provider used by `llm/embed`. The provider keyword may be `:jina`, `:voyage`, `:cohere`, or any other (treated as OpenAI-compatible). The map carries `:api-key`, optional `:default-model`/`:model`, and `:base-url` for OpenAI-compatible endpoints. Returns nil.

```sema
(llm/configure-embeddings :openai {:api-key "sk-..." :model "text-embedding-3-small"})
```

entries.name: llm/current-provider
entries.summary: Return information about the active default provider as a map with `:name` (keyword) and `:model` (the provider's default model string). Returns nil if no provider is configured.
entries.body:
Return information about the active default provider as a map with `:name` (keyword) and `:model` (the provider's default model string). Returns nil if no provider is configured.

```sema
(llm/current-provider)   ; => {:name :anthropic :model "claude-sonnet-4-6"}
```

entries.name: llm/default-provider
entries.summary: Return the name of the current default provider as a keyword, or nil if none is configured.
entries.body:
Return the name of the current default provider as a keyword, or nil if none is configured.

```sema
(llm/default-provider)   ; => :anthropic
```

entries.name: llm/define-provider
entries.summary: Define a custom LLM provider implemented in Sema. The map must include a `:complete` function (lambda or native fn) that receives a request map and returns a string or a response map (with `:content`, optional `:role`, `:model`, `:usage`, `:tool-calls`); `:default-model` is optional. Registers the provider and makes it the default, returning its name keyword.
entries.body:
Define a custom LLM provider implemented in Sema. The map must include a `:complete` function (lambda or native fn) that receives a request map and returns a string or a response map (with `:content`, optional `:role`, `:model`, `:usage`, `:tool-calls`); `:default-model` is optional. Registers the provider and makes it the default, returning its name keyword.

```sema
(llm/define-provider :echo
  {:complete (fn [req] {:content (string-append "echo: " (:content (first (:messages req))))})
   :default-model "echo-1"})
```

entries.name: llm/embed
entries.summary: Compute embeddings using the configured embedding provider. Given a single string, returns one embedding as a bytevector (f64 little-endian floats); given a list of strings, returns a list of bytevectors. The opts map accepts `:model`. Use the `embedding/*` helpers to convert to/from float lists.
entries.body:
Compute embeddings using the configured embedding provider. Given a single string, returns one embedding as a bytevector (f64 little-endian floats); given a list of strings, returns a list of bytevectors. The opts map accepts `:model`. Use the `embedding/*` helpers to convert to/from float lists.

```sema
(llm/embed "the quick brown fox")          ; => #bytevector(...)
(llm/embed ["alpha" "beta"] {:model "text-embedding-3-small"})
```

entries.name: llm/extract
entries.summary: Extract structured data from text into a value matching the given schema. The model is instructed to return JSON-only; the response is parsed and (by default) validated against the schema, with automatic retries. The opts map accepts `:model`, `:validate` (default true), `:retries` (default 2), and `:reask?` (default true, sends the prior response and validation error back to the model). Returns the extracted value.
entries.body:
Extract structured data from text into a value matching the given schema. The model is instructed to return JSON-only; the response is parsed and (by default) validated against the schema, with automatic retries. The opts map accepts `:model`, `:validate` (default true), `:retries` (default 2), and `:reask?` (default true, sends the prior response and validation error back to the model). Returns the extracted value.

```sema
(llm/extract {:name :string :age :int} "John Doe is 42 years old" {:retries 1})
```

entries.name: llm/extract-from-image
entries.summary: Extract structured data from an image into a value matching the given schema. The source is either a file-path string or a bytevector of image bytes; the media type is auto-detected. The model is asked to return JSON only, which is parsed into a Sema value. The opts map accepts `:model`.
entries.body:
Extract structured data from an image into a value matching the given schema. The source is either a file-path string or a bytevector of image bytes; the media type is auto-detected. The model is asked to return JSON only, which is parsed into a Sema value. The opts map accepts `:model`.

```sema
(llm/extract-from-image {:total :number :date :string} "receipt.png")
```

entries.name: llm/last-usage
entries.summary: Return token usage for the most recent LLM call as a map with `:prompt-tokens`, `:completion-tokens`, `:total-tokens`, `:model`, and (when pricing is known) `:cost-usd`. Returns nil if no call has been made yet.
entries.body:
Return token usage for the most recent LLM call as a map with `:prompt-tokens`, `:completion-tokens`, `:total-tokens`, `:model`, and (when pricing is known) `:cost-usd`. Returns nil if no call has been made yet.

```sema
(llm/complete "hi")
(llm/last-usage)   ; => {:prompt-tokens 8 :completion-tokens 12 :total-tokens 20 :model "..."}
```

entries.name: llm/list-providers
entries.summary: Return a list of keywords naming all currently configured providers.
entries.body:
Return a list of keywords naming all currently configured providers.

```sema
(llm/list-providers)   ; => (:anthropic :ollama)
```

entries.name: llm/pmap
entries.summary: Map a function over a collection to produce prompt strings, then send all prompts to the default provider in parallel (via the provider's batch completion). Each item is passed to `fn` to build its prompt; the results are returned as a list of completion strings in input order. The opts map accepts `:model`, `:max-tokens`, `:temperature`, and `:system`.
entries.body:
Map a function over a collection to produce prompt strings, then send all prompts to the default provider in parallel (via the provider's batch completion). Each item is passed to `fn` to build its prompt; the results are returned as a list of completion strings in input order. The opts map accepts `:model`, `:max-tokens`, `:temperature`, and `:system`.

```sema
(llm/pmap (fn [topic] (string-append "Define: " topic))
          ["entropy" "recursion" "monad"]
          {:max-tokens 60})
```

entries.name: llm/pricing-status
entries.summary: Return the status of the pricing table used for cost estimates: a map with `:source` (a symbol indicating where pricing came from) and, when available, `:updated-at` (a date string).
entries.body:
Return the status of the pricing table used for cost estimates: a map with `:source` (a symbol indicating where pricing came from) and, when available, `:updated-at` (a date string).

```sema
(llm/pricing-status)   ; => {:source hardcoded}
```

entries.name: llm/providers
entries.summary: Return a list of keywords naming all currently configured providers. Equivalent to `llm/list-providers`.
entries.body:
Return a list of keywords naming all currently configured providers. Equivalent to `llm/list-providers`.

```sema
(llm/providers)   ; => (:anthropic :ollama)
```

entries.name: llm/rerank
entries.summary: Reorder `documents` (a list of strings) by their cross-encoder relevance to `query`, using a hosted reranking provider (Cohere, Jina, or Voyage — the same **API key** you use for embeddings, e.g. `COHERE_API_KEY` / `JINA_API_KEY` / `VOYAGE_API_KEY`). Unlike cosine similarity over embeddings, a reranker reads the query and each document *together*, so it is far more precise — the standard RAG move is to retrieve many candidates by vector search, then rerank to the best few.
examples:
(llm/rerank "how do I read a file?"
            (list "vectors are cool" "use file/read to read a file" "unrelated trivia")
            {:top-k 2})
;; => ({:index 1 :score 0.91 :document "use file/read to read a file"} ...)
entries.body:
Reorder `documents` (a list of strings) by their cross-encoder relevance to `query`, using a hosted reranking provider (Cohere, Jina, or Voyage — the same **API key** you use for embeddings, e.g. `COHERE_API_KEY` / `JINA_API_KEY` / `VOYAGE_API_KEY`). Unlike cosine similarity over embeddings, a reranker reads the query and each document *together*, so it is far more precise — the standard RAG move is to retrieve many candidates by vector search, then rerank to the best few.

Returns a list of maps `{:index :score :document}`, highest relevance first. `:index` is the position in the original `documents` list, `:score` the relevance (higher is better; scores are query-dependent, use them for ordering, not as calibrated probabilities).

The opts map accepts `:top-k` (keep only the K best), `:model` (override the provider's default reranker), and `:provider` (`:cohere` / `:jina` / `:voyage` — defaults to the configured rerank provider).

```sema
(llm/rerank "how do I read a file?"
            (list "vectors are cool" "use file/read to read a file" "unrelated trivia")
            {:top-k 2})
;; => ({:index 1 :score 0.91 :document "use file/read to read a file"} ...)
```

See the [RAG guide](/docs/llm/rag) for an end-to-end retrieve → rerank → answer pipeline.

entries.name: llm/reset-usage
entries.summary: Reset session usage tracking: zeros the accumulated prompt/completion token counts, clears the last-usage record, and resets session cost to zero.
entries.body:
Reset session usage tracking: zeros the accumulated prompt/completion token counts, clears the last-usage record, and resets session cost to zero.

```sema
(llm/reset-usage)
```

entries.name: llm/send
entries.summary: Send a structured prompt value (built with the prompt helpers) to the default provider and return the completion. The first argument must be a prompt value; the optional opts map carries model/generation options.
examples:
(llm/send (prompt (system "You are a helpful assistant.")
                  (user "Summarize the plot of Hamlet in one line."))
          {:max-tokens 80})
entries.body:
Send a structured prompt value (built with the prompt helpers) to the default provider and return the completion. The first argument must be a prompt value; the optional opts map carries model/generation options.

```sema
(llm/send (prompt (system "You are a helpful assistant.")
                  (user "Summarize the plot of Hamlet in one line."))
          {:max-tokens 80})
```

entries.name: llm/session-usage
entries.summary: Return cumulative token usage for the whole session as a map with `:prompt-tokens`, `:completion-tokens`, `:total-tokens`, and `:cost-usd`. The totals accumulate across all LLM calls until reset with `llm/reset-usage`.
entries.body:
Return cumulative token usage for the whole session as a map with `:prompt-tokens`, `:completion-tokens`, `:total-tokens`, and `:cost-usd`. The totals accumulate across all LLM calls until reset with `llm/reset-usage`.

```sema
(llm/session-usage)   ; => {:prompt-tokens 120 :completion-tokens 340 :total-tokens 460 :cost-usd 0.0021}
```

entries.name: llm/set-budget
entries.summary: Set a global spending budget (in USD) for LLM calls and reset the amount spent to zero. Subsequent calls accumulate cost; exceeding the limit raises an error. Costs are estimated from the pricing table, so enforcement is best-effort when pricing is unknown.
entries.body:
Set a global spending budget (in USD) for LLM calls and reset the amount spent to zero. Subsequent calls accumulate cost; exceeding the limit raises an error. Costs are estimated from the pricing table, so enforcement is best-effort when pricing is unknown.

```sema
(llm/set-budget 0.50)
```

entries.name: llm/set-default
entries.summary: Set which already-configured provider is the default for chat calls. The argument is a provider keyword or string. Errors if the named provider is not configured. Returns the provider name as a keyword.
entries.body:
Set which already-configured provider is the default for chat calls. The argument is a provider keyword or string. Errors if the named provider is not configured. Returns the provider name as a keyword.

```sema
(llm/set-default :openai)
```

entries.name: llm/set-pricing
entries.summary: Register custom pricing for cost calculation. The model pattern matches model names; the two numbers are the input and output costs per million tokens (USD). Used to compute `:cost-usd` in usage reports.
entries.body:
Register custom pricing for cost calculation. The model pattern matches model names; the two numbers are the input and output costs per million tokens (USD). Used to compute `:cost-usd` in usage reports.

```sema
(llm/set-pricing "my-model" 0.5 1.5)
```

entries.name: llm/similarity
entries.summary: Compute the cosine similarity between two embeddings. Both arguments must be the same type: either two embedding bytevectors (f64 little-endian, length a multiple of 8) or two lists of numbers of equal length. Returns a float; 0.0 when either vector has zero magnitude.
entries.body:
Compute the cosine similarity between two embeddings. Both arguments must be the same type: either two embedding bytevectors (f64 little-endian, length a multiple of 8) or two lists of numbers of equal length. Returns a float; 0.0 when either vector has zero magnitude.

```sema
(llm/similarity (llm/embed "cat") (llm/embed "kitten"))   ; => ~0.89
```

entries.name: llm/stream
entries.summary: Stream a completion from the default provider. The first argument is a prompt string, a prompt value, or a messages sequence. An optional function argument is invoked with each text chunk; without a callback, chunks are printed to stdout. An optional opts map accepts `:model`, `:max-tokens`, `:temperature`, and `:system`. Returns the full accumulated response string.
entries.body:
Stream a completion from the default provider. The first argument is a prompt string, a prompt value, or a messages sequence. An optional function argument is invoked with each text chunk; without a callback, chunks are printed to stdout. An optional opts map accepts `:model`, `:max-tokens`, `:temperature`, and `:system`. Returns the full accumulated response string.

```sema
(llm/stream "Tell me a short story" (fn [chunk] (display chunk)) {:max-tokens 200})
```

entries.name: llm/summarize
entries.summary: Summarize a block of text using the default provider. The opts map accepts `:model`, `:max-length` (target word count), and `:style` (`"paragraph"` default, `"bullet-points"`/`"bullets"`, or `"one-line"`). Returns the summary string.
entries.body:
Summarize a block of text using the default provider. The opts map accepts `:model`, `:max-length` (target word count), and `:style` (`"paragraph"` default, `"bullet-points"`/`"bullets"`, or `"one-line"`). Returns the summary string.

```sema
(llm/summarize long-article {:style "bullet-points" :max-length 100})
```

entries.name: llm/token-count
entries.summary: Estimate the number of tokens in a string (or the combined length of a list of strings) using a simple chars-divided-by-4 heuristic. Returns an integer token estimate.
entries.body:
Estimate the number of tokens in a string (or the combined length of a list of strings) using a simple chars-divided-by-4 heuristic. Returns an integer token estimate.

```sema
(llm/token-count "the quick brown fox jumps")   ; => 6
```

entries.name: llm/token-estimate
entries.summary: Estimate tokens for a string and return details as a map with `:tokens` (the chars/4 estimate), `:method` (`"chars/4"`), and `:chars` (the character count).
entries.body:
Estimate tokens for a string and return details as a map with `:tokens` (the chars/4 estimate), `:method` (`"chars/4"`), and `:chars` (the character count).

```sema
(llm/token-estimate "hello world")   ; => {:tokens 2 :method "chars/4" :chars 11}
```

entries.name: llm/with-budget
entries.summary: Run a zero-argument function under a scoped budget. The opts map requires at least `:max-cost-usd` and/or `:max-tokens`; the scope is pushed before calling the thunk and restored afterward (even on error). LLM calls inside the thunk count against the scoped limit. Returns the thunk's result.
entries.body:
Run a zero-argument function under a scoped budget. The opts map requires at least `:max-cost-usd` and/or `:max-tokens`; the scope is pushed before calling the thunk and restored afterward (even on error). LLM calls inside the thunk count against the scoped limit. Returns the thunk's result.

```sema
(llm/with-budget {:max-cost-usd 0.10}
  (fn [] (llm/complete "summarize this in one line")))
```

entries.name: llm/with-cache
entries.summary: Run a zero-argument function with LLM response caching enabled for its duration. With two arguments, the first is an opts map accepting `:ttl` (cache time-to-live in seconds, default 3600); with one argument it is just the thunk. The previous cache settings are restored after the call. Returns the thunk's result.
entries.body:
Run a zero-argument function with LLM response caching enabled for its duration. With two arguments, the first is an opts map accepting `:ttl` (cache time-to-live in seconds, default 3600); with one argument it is just the thunk. The previous cache settings are restored after the call. Returns the thunk's result.

```sema
(llm/with-cache {:ttl 600}
  (fn [] (llm/complete "what is 2+2?")))
```

entries.name: llm/with-cassette
entries.summary: Record or replay LLM calls against a tape file for the duration of a zero-argument function — for deterministic, keyless tests and reproducible demos. `:mode` is `:auto` (default — replay if recorded, else record), `:record`, or `:replay` (a miss is a hard error). The opts map is optional. The response cache is disabled for the scope, and the tape is flushed on exit. Covers `llm/complete`, `llm/chat`, `llm/embed`, `llm/stream`, and agent loops. Returns the thunk's result.
entries.body:
Record or replay LLM calls against a tape file for the duration of a zero-argument function — for deterministic, keyless tests and reproducible demos. `:mode` is `:auto` (default — replay if recorded, else record), `:record`, or `:replay` (a miss is a hard error). The opts map is optional. The response cache is disabled for the scope, and the tape is flushed on exit. Covers `llm/complete`, `llm/chat`, `llm/embed`, `llm/stream`, and agent loops. Returns the thunk's result.

```sema
(llm/with-cassette "tapes/greeting.jsonl" {:mode :auto}
  (fn () (llm/complete "Say hello in one word." {:model "gpt-5-mini"})))
```

entries.name: llm/with-fallback
entries.summary: Run a zero-argument function with a fallback provider chain in effect. The first argument is a list of provider keywords/strings to try in order if the primary provider fails. The previous fallback chain is restored after the call. Returns the thunk's result.
entries.body:
Run a zero-argument function with a fallback provider chain in effect. The first argument is a list of provider keywords/strings to try in order if the primary provider fails. The previous fallback chain is restored after the call. Returns the thunk's result.

```sema
(llm/with-fallback [:anthropic :openai :ollama]
  (fn [] (llm/complete "draft a one-line tagline")))
```

entries.name: llm/with-rate-limit
entries.summary: Run a zero-argument function with a requests-per-second rate limit in effect for LLM calls. The first argument is the allowed rate (requests per second). The previous rate-limit setting is restored after the call. Returns the thunk's result.
entries.body:
Run a zero-argument function with a requests-per-second rate limit in effect for LLM calls. The first argument is the allowed rate (requests per second). The previous rate-limit setting is restored after the call. Returns the thunk's result.

```sema
(llm/with-rate-limit 2
  (fn [] (llm/batch ["q1" "q2" "q3"])))
```

entries.name: log/debug
entries.summary: Write a `[DEBUG]` log line to stderr. Accepts one or more values, joined by spaces (strings as-is, others stringified). Any active logging context is appended.
entries.body:
Write a `[DEBUG]` log line to stderr. Accepts one or more values, joined by spaces (strings as-is, others stringified). Any active logging context is appended.

```sema
(log/debug "request payload" payload)
```

entries.name: log/error
entries.summary: Write an `[ERROR]` log line to stderr. Accepts one or more values, joined by spaces (strings as-is, others stringified). Any active logging context is appended.
entries.body:
Write an `[ERROR]` log line to stderr. Accepts one or more values, joined by spaces (strings as-is, others stringified). Any active logging context is appended.

```sema
(log/error "failed to connect:" err)
```

entries.name: log/info
entries.summary: Write an `[INFO]` log line to stderr. Accepts one or more values, joined by spaces (strings as-is, others stringified). Any active logging context is appended.
entries.body:
Write an `[INFO]` log line to stderr. Accepts one or more values, joined by spaces (strings as-is, others stringified). Any active logging context is appended.

```sema
(log/info "server started on port" 8080)
```

entries.name: log/warn
entries.summary: Write a `[WARN]` log line to stderr. Accepts one or more values, joined by spaces (strings as-is, others stringified). Any active logging context is appended.
entries.body:
Write a `[WARN]` log line to stderr. Accepts one or more values, joined by spaces (strings as-is, others stringified). Any active logging context is appended.

```sema
(log/warn "retrying request, attempt" n)
```

entries.name: assoc
entries.summary: Add or update a key-value pair, returning a new map.

entries.name: assoc-in
entries.summary: Return a copy of `m` with `value` set at the nested key `path`, creating intermediate maps as needed. Same as `map/assoc-in`.

entries.name: contains?
entries.summary: Test if a map contains a key.
entries.body:
Test if a map contains a key.

```sema
(contains? {:a 1} :a)   ; => #t
(contains? {:a 1} :b)   ; => #f
```

entries.name: count
entries.summary: Return the number of key-value pairs.
entries.body:
Return the number of key-value pairs.

```sema
(count {:a 1 :b 2})   ; => 2
```

entries.name: deep-merge
entries.summary: Recursively merge maps left to right. Nested maps are merged; non-map values from later arguments overwrite earlier ones. Same as `map/deep-merge`. The result is always an ordered map, regardless of the input map types (unlike `merge`, which preserves the type of its first argument).

entries.name: dissoc
entries.summary: Remove a key, returning a new map. Works on both maps and hashmaps.
entries.body:
Remove a key, returning a new map. Works on both maps and hashmaps.

```sema
(dissoc {:a 1 :b 2} :a)                     ; => {:b 2}
(dissoc (hashmap/new :a 1 :b 2) :a)         ; hashmap without :a
```

entries.name: get
entries.summary: Look up a value by key. Works on both maps and hashmaps.

params.name: default
params.doc: optional value returned when key is absent (defaults to nil)

entries.body:
Look up a value by key. Works on both maps and hashmaps.

```sema
(get {:a 1 :b 2} :a)   ; => 1
(get {:a 1 :b 2} :z)   ; => nil
```

entries.name: get-in
entries.summary: Access a value at a nested key path. Returns `nil` (or `default`) if any key along the path is missing. Same as `map/get-in`.
entries.body:
Access a value at a nested key path. Returns `nil` (or `default`) if any key along the path is missing. Same as `map/get-in`.

```sema
(get-in {:a {:b {:c 42}}} [:a :b :c])         ; => 42
(get-in {:a {:b 1}} [:a :c])                   ; => nil
(get-in {:a {:b 1}} [:a :c] "default")         ; => "default"
```

entries.name: hash-map
entries.summary: Construct a map from alternating key/value arguments. Requires an even number of arguments. Alias of `map/new`.

entries.name: hash-map?
entries.summary: Return `#t` if `x` is a map. Alias of `map?`.
entries.body:
Return `#t` if `x` is a map. Alias of `map?`.

```sema
(hash-map? {:a 1})   ; => #t
(hash-map? '(1 2))   ; => #f
```

entries.name: hash-ref
entries.summary: Look up `key` in map `m`, returning the associated value or `default` (`nil` if omitted) when the key is missing. Alias of `get`.
entries.body:
Look up `key` in map `m`, returning the associated value or `default` (`nil` if omitted) when the key is missing. Alias of `get`.

```sema
(hash-ref {:a 1 :b 2} :a)         ; => 1
(hash-ref {:a 1} :missing)        ; => nil
(hash-ref {:a 1} :missing "none") ; => "none"
```

entries.name: hashmap/assoc
entries.summary: Add a key-value pair to a hashmap.
entries.body:
Add a key-value pair to a hashmap.

```sema
(hashmap/assoc (hashmap/new) :a 1)   ; hashmap with :a 1
```

entries.name: hashmap/contains?
entries.summary: Test if a hashmap contains a key.
entries.body:
Test if a hashmap contains a key.

```sema
(hashmap/contains? (hashmap/new :a 1) :a)   ; => #t
```

entries.name: hashmap/get
entries.summary: Look up a value in a hashmap.
entries.body:
Look up a value in a hashmap.

```sema
(hashmap/get (hashmap/new :a 1) :a)   ; => 1
```

entries.name: hashmap/keys
entries.summary: Return the keys of a hashmap (unordered).
entries.body:
Return the keys of a hashmap (unordered).

```sema
(hashmap/keys (hashmap/new :a 1 :b 2))   ; => (:a :b)
```

entries.name: hashmap/new
entries.summary: Create a new hashmap from key-value pairs.
entries.body:
Create a new hashmap from key-value pairs.

```sema
(hashmap/new :a 1 :b 2 :c 3)   ; create a hashmap
(hashmap/new)                    ; empty hashmap
```

entries.name: hashmap/to-map
entries.summary: Convert a hashmap to a sorted map.
entries.body:
Convert a hashmap to a sorted map.

```sema
(hashmap/to-map (hashmap/new :b 2 :a 1))   ; => {:a 1 :b 2}
```

entries.name: keys
entries.summary: Return the keys of a map as a list.
entries.body:
Return the keys of a map as a list.

```sema
(keys {:a 1 :b 2})   ; => (:a :b)
```

entries.name: map/assoc-in
entries.summary: Set a value at a nested key path. Creates intermediate maps if they don't exist.

entries.name: map/deep-merge
entries.summary: Recursively merge maps. Nested maps are merged rather than replaced. Non-map values in the overlay override the base.

entries.name: map/entries
entries.summary: Return the entries as a list of key-value pairs.
entries.body:
Return the entries as a list of key-value pairs.

```sema
(map/entries {:a 1 :b 2})   ; => ((:a 1) (:b 2))
```

entries.name: map/except
entries.summary: Remove specified keys from a map (inverse of `map/select-keys`).

entries.name: map/filter
entries.summary: Filter entries by a predicate that takes key and value.
entries.body:
Filter entries by a predicate that takes key and value.

```sema
(map/filter (fn (k v) (> v 1)) {:a 1 :b 2 :c 3})   ; => {:b 2 :c 3}
```

entries.name: map/from-entries
entries.summary: Create a map from a list of key-value pairs.
entries.body:
Create a map from a list of key-value pairs.

```sema
(map/from-entries '((:a 1) (:b 2)))   ; => {:a 1 :b 2}
```

entries.name: map/get-in
entries.summary: Access a value at a nested key path. Returns `nil` (or a default) if any key is missing.
entries.body:
Access a value at a nested key path. Returns `nil` (or a default) if any key is missing.

```sema
(map/get-in {:a {:b {:c 42}}} [:a :b :c])           ; => 42
(map/get-in {:a {:b 1}} [:a :c])                     ; => nil
(map/get-in {:a {:b 1}} [:a :c] "default")           ; => "default"
```

entries.name: map/map-keys
entries.summary: Apply a function to every key in a map.
entries.body:
Apply a function to every key in a map.

```sema
(map/map-keys
  (fn (k) (string/to-keyword (string/upper (keyword/to-string k))))
  {:a 1})
; => {:A 1}
```

entries.name: map/map-vals
entries.summary: Apply a function to every value in a map.
entries.body:
Apply a function to every value in a map.

```sema
(map/map-vals (fn (v) (* v 2)) {:a 1 :b 2})   ; => {:a 2 :b 4}
```

entries.name: map/new
entries.summary: Create a map from key-value pairs.
entries.body:
Create a map from key-value pairs.

```sema
(map/new :a 1 :b 2)   ; => {:a 1 :b 2}
```

entries.name: map/select-keys
entries.summary: Select only the given keys from a map.
entries.body:
Select only the given keys from a map.

```sema
(map/select-keys {:a 1 :b 2 :c 3} '(:a :c))   ; => {:a 1 :c 3}
```

entries.name: map/sort-keys
entries.summary: Sort a map by its keys. Converts hashmaps to sorted maps.
entries.body:
Sort a map by its keys. Converts hashmaps to sorted maps.

```sema
(map/sort-keys (hashmap/new :c 3 :a 1 :b 2))   ; => {:a 1 :b 2 :c 3}
```

entries.name: map/update
entries.summary: Update a value at a key by applying a function.
entries.body:
Update a value at a key by applying a function.

```sema
(map/update {:a 1} :a (fn (v) (+ v 10)))   ; => {:a 11}
```

entries.name: map/update-in
entries.summary: Update a value at a nested key path by applying a function.
entries.body:
Update a value at a nested key path by applying a function.

```sema
(map/update-in {:a {:b 10}} [:a :b] #(+ % 1))       ; => {:a {:b 11}}
```

entries.name: map/zip
entries.summary: Create a map from a list of keys and a list of values.
entries.body:
Create a map from a list of keys and a list of values.

```sema
(map/zip '(:a :b :c) '(1 2 3))   ; => {:a 1 :b 2 :c 3}
```

entries.name: merge
entries.summary: Merge multiple maps together. Later maps override earlier ones. Works on both maps and hashmaps — the result type matches the first argument.

entries.name: update-in
entries.summary: Return a copy of `m` with the value at the nested key `path` replaced by `(f current)`, where `current` is the existing value (`nil` if missing). Same as `map/update-in`.
entries.body:
Return a copy of `m` with the value at the nested key `path` replaced by `(f current)`, where `current` is the existing value (`nil` if missing). Same as `map/update-in`.

```sema
(update-in {:a {:b 1}} [:a :b] (fn (x) (+ x 10)))   ; => {:a {:b 11}}
```

entries.name: vals
entries.summary: Return the values of a map as a list.
entries.body:
Return the values of a map as a list.

```sema
(vals {:a 1 :b 2})   ; => (1 2)
```

entries.summary: Multiply numbers together.
entries.body:
Multiply numbers together.

```sema
(* 4 5)       ; => 20
(* 2 3 4)     ; => 24
(*)           ; => 1
```

entries.summary: Add numbers together. Accepts any number of arguments.
entries.body:
Add numbers together. Accepts any number of arguments.

```sema
(+ 1 2 3)     ; => 6
(+ 10)        ; => 10
(+)           ; => 0
```

entries.summary: Subtract numbers. With one argument, negates. With multiple, subtracts left to right.
entries.body:
Subtract numbers. With one argument, negates. With multiple, subtracts left to right.

```sema
(- 10 3)      ; => 7
(- 10 3 2)    ; => 5
(- 5)         ; => -5
```

entries.summary: Divide numbers. Returns a float when the division is not exact (so `(/ 10 3)` is `3.3333...`, not `3`). For truncated integer division use [`math/quotient`](#math-quotient).
entries.body:
Divide numbers. Returns a float when the division is not exact (so `(/ 10 3)` is `3.3333...`, not `3`). For truncated integer division use [`math/quotient`](#math-quotient).

```sema
(/ 10 2)      ;; => 5
(/ 10 3)      ;; => 3.3333333333333335
(/ 10.0 3)    ;; => 3.3333333333333335
```

entries.summary: Less than. Supports chaining.
entries.body:
Less than. Supports chaining.

```sema
(< 1 2)       ; => #t
(< 1 2 3)     ; => #t
(< 3 2)       ; => #f
```

entries.summary: Less than or equal.
entries.body:
Less than or equal.

```sema
(<= 1 2)      ; => #t
(<= 2 2)      ; => #t
```

entries.summary: Equality. For numbers this is numeric equality (so `(= 1 1.0)` is `#t`); for non-numbers it falls back to structural equality. Unlike `<` / `>`, comparing non-numbers does not error.
entries.body:
Equality. For numbers this is numeric equality (so `(= 1 1.0)` is `#t`); for
non-numbers it falls back to structural equality. Unlike `<` / `>`, comparing
non-numbers does not error.

```sema
(= 1 1)           ; => #t
(= 1 1.0)         ; => #t
(= 1 2)           ; => #f
(= "abc" "abc")   ; => #t   (structural, not an error)
```

entries.summary: Greater than.
entries.body:
Greater than.

```sema
(> 3 2)       ; => #t
(> 1 2)       ; => #f
```

entries.summary: Greater than or equal.
entries.body:
Greater than or equal.

```sema
(>= 3 2)      ; => #t
(>= 2 2)      ; => #t
```

entries.name: abs
entries.summary: Absolute value.
entries.body:
Absolute value.

```sema
(abs -5)      ; => 5
(abs 3)       ; => 3
(abs -3.14)   ; => 3.14
```

entries.name: bit/and
entries.summary: Bitwise AND.
entries.body:
Bitwise AND.

```sema
(bit/and 5 3)      ; => 1
(bit/and 15 9)     ; => 9
```

entries.name: bit/not
entries.summary: Bitwise NOT (complement).
entries.body:
Bitwise NOT (complement).

```sema
(bit/not 5)        ; => -6
```

entries.name: bit/or
entries.summary: Bitwise OR.
entries.body:
Bitwise OR.

```sema
(bit/or 5 3)       ; => 7
(bit/or 8 4)       ; => 12
```

entries.name: bit/shift-left
entries.summary: Left bit shift.
entries.body:
Left bit shift.

```sema
(bit/shift-left 1 4)   ; => 16
(bit/shift-left 3 2)   ; => 12
```

entries.name: bit/shift-right
entries.summary: Right bit shift.
entries.body:
Right bit shift.

```sema
(bit/shift-right 16 2) ; => 4
(bit/shift-right 8 1)  ; => 4
```

entries.name: bit/xor
entries.summary: Bitwise XOR.
entries.body:
Bitwise XOR.

```sema
(bit/xor 5 3)      ; => 6
```

entries.name: ceil
entries.summary: Round up to nearest integer.
entries.body:
Round up to nearest integer.

```sema
(ceil 3.2)    ; => 4
(ceil -2.7)   ; => -2
```

entries.name: ceiling
entries.summary: Alias for `ceil`.
entries.body:
Alias for `ceil`.

```sema
(ceiling 3.2)  ; => 4
```

entries.name: cos
entries.summary: Cosine (argument in radians).
entries.body:
Cosine (argument in radians).

```sema
(cos 0)       ; => 1.0
(cos pi)      ; => -1.0
```

entries.name: e
entries.summary: Euler's number (2.71828...).

entries.name: even?
entries.summary: Test if an integer is even.
entries.body:
Test if an integer is even.

```sema
(even? 4)      ; => #t
(even? 3)      ; => #f
```

entries.name: expt
entries.summary: Alias for `pow` (Scheme name for exponentiation).
entries.body:
Alias for `pow` (Scheme name for exponentiation).

```sema
(expt 2 10)   ; => 1024
```

entries.name: float
entries.summary: Convert a number or numeric string to a float. Signals an error if a string cannot be parsed as a float.
entries.body:
Convert a number or numeric string to a float. Signals an error if a string cannot be parsed as a float.

```sema
(float 5)      ; => 5.0
(float "3.5")  ; => 3.5
```

entries.name: floor
entries.summary: Round down to nearest integer.
entries.body:
Round down to nearest integer.

```sema
(floor 3.7)   ; => 3
(floor -2.3)  ; => -3
```

entries.name: int
entries.summary: Convert a number or numeric string to an integer. Floats are truncated toward zero. Signals an error if a string cannot be parsed as an integer.
entries.body:
Convert a number or numeric string to an integer. Floats are truncated toward zero. Signals an error if a string cannot be parsed as an integer.

```sema
(int 3.9)    ; => 3
(int "42")   ; => 42
```

entries.name: log
entries.summary: Natural logarithm.
entries.body:
Natural logarithm.

```sema
(log 1)       ; => 0.0
(log 100)     ; => 4.605...
```

entries.name: math/acos
entries.summary: Inverse cosine. Returns radians.
entries.body:
Inverse cosine. Returns radians.

```sema
(math/acos 0)      ; => ~1.5707 (π/2)
(math/acos 1)      ; => 0.0
```

entries.name: math/asin
entries.summary: Inverse sine. Returns radians.
entries.body:
Inverse sine. Returns radians.

```sema
(math/asin 1)      ; => ~1.5707 (π/2)
(math/asin 0)      ; => 0.0
```

entries.name: math/atan
entries.summary: Inverse tangent. Returns radians.
entries.body:
Inverse tangent. Returns radians.

```sema
(math/atan 1)      ; => ~0.7854 (π/4)
(math/atan 0)      ; => 0.0
```

entries.name: math/atan2
entries.summary: Two-argument inverse tangent. Returns the angle in radians between the positive x-axis and the point (x, y).
entries.body:
Two-argument inverse tangent. Returns the angle in radians between the positive x-axis and the point (x, y).

```sema
(math/atan2 1 1)   ; => ~0.7854 (π/4)
(math/atan2 0 -1)  ; => ~3.1416 (π)
```

entries.name: math/clamp
entries.summary: Clamp a value to a range.
entries.body:
Clamp a value to a range.

```sema
(math/clamp 15 0 10)   ; => 10
(math/clamp -5 0 10)   ; => 0
(math/clamp 5 0 10)    ; => 5
```

entries.name: math/cosh
entries.summary: Hyperbolic cosine.
entries.body:
Hyperbolic cosine.

```sema
(math/cosh 0)      ; => 1.0
(math/cosh 1)      ; => 1.5430...
```

entries.name: otel/event
entries.summary: Add an event (with optional attributes) to the current OpenTelemetry span. Attribute values are stringified. Returns nil. A no-op when telemetry is disabled or when there is no active span.
entries.body:
Add an event (with optional attributes) to the current OpenTelemetry span. Attribute values are stringified. Returns nil. A no-op when telemetry is disabled or when there is no active span.

**Signature:** `(otel/event name) → nil` · `(otel/event name attrs-map) → nil`

```sema
(otel/span "request"
  (fn ()
    (otel/event "cache-miss" {:key "user:42"})
    (fetch-user 42)))
```

Telemetry is opt-in: events are only exported when an OTLP endpoint or `SEMA_OTEL_FILE` is configured. See the [Observability guide](/docs/llm/observability).

entries.name: otel/llm-span
entries.summary: Run a thunk inside a typed **LLM/generation** span for an LLM call you make yourself (a provider Sema doesn't natively support). The config map supplies `:model`, `:provider`, and `:operation` (default `"chat"`); any other keys become span attributes. Sets the `gen_ai.*` request attributes and, when `SEMA_OTEL_COMPAT` is set, the backend-native span-kind — so the call renders as a first-class generation in Phoenix/Traceloop/Langfuse. Account tokens with `otel/llm-usage` inside the thunk. A no-op when telemetry is disabled.
entries.body:
Run a thunk inside a typed **LLM/generation** span for an LLM call you make yourself (a provider Sema doesn't natively support). The config map supplies `:model`, `:provider`, and `:operation` (default `"chat"`); any other keys become span attributes. Sets the `gen_ai.*` request attributes and, when `SEMA_OTEL_COMPAT` is set, the backend-native span-kind — so the call renders as a first-class generation in Phoenix/Traceloop/Langfuse. Account tokens with `otel/llm-usage` inside the thunk. A no-op when telemetry is disabled.

**Signature:** `(otel/llm-span config-map thunk) → any`

```sema
(otel/llm-span {:model "custom-model" :provider "myco" :operation "chat"}
  (fn ()
    (let ((resp (my-http-llm-call prompt)))
      (otel/llm-usage {:input-tokens 120 :output-tokens 30 :cost-usd 0.001})
      resp)))
```

See the [Observability guide](/docs/llm/observability).

entries.name: otel/llm-usage
entries.summary: Record LLM token usage and cost on the innermost active span (typically inside an `otel/llm-span`). Emits the same `gen_ai.usage.*` keys as the built-in `llm/*` path plus the active backend's compat aliases, so a custom-provider call accounts identically. Map keys: `:input-tokens`, `:output-tokens`, `:cost-usd`. A no-op when telemetry is disabled or there is no active span.
entries.body:
Record LLM token usage and cost on the innermost active span (typically inside an `otel/llm-span`). Emits the same `gen_ai.usage.*` keys as the built-in `llm/*` path plus the active backend's compat aliases, so a custom-provider call accounts identically. Map keys: `:input-tokens`, `:output-tokens`, `:cost-usd`. A no-op when telemetry is disabled or there is no active span.

**Signature:** `(otel/llm-usage usage-map) → nil`

```sema
(otel/llm-span {:model "custom-model" :provider "myco"}
  (fn ()
    (otel/llm-usage {:input-tokens 120 :output-tokens 30 :cost-usd 0.001})
    "done"))
```

See the [Observability guide](/docs/llm/observability).

entries.name: otel/retrieval-span
entries.summary: Run a thunk inside a typed **RETRIEVER** span for a retrieval / vector-search step you build yourself. When `SEMA_OTEL_COMPAT` is set it emits the backend-native RETRIEVER span-kind, so a custom RAG step renders like the built-in `vector-store/search` span in Phoenix/Langfuse. Optional third arg is an attributes map. A no-op when telemetry is disabled.
entries.body:
Run a thunk inside a typed **RETRIEVER** span for a retrieval / vector-search step you build yourself. When `SEMA_OTEL_COMPAT` is set it emits the backend-native RETRIEVER span-kind, so a custom RAG step renders like the built-in `vector-store/search` span in Phoenix/Langfuse. Optional third arg is an attributes map. A no-op when telemetry is disabled.

**Signature:** `(otel/retrieval-span name thunk) → any` · `(otel/retrieval-span name thunk attrs) → any`

```sema
(otel/retrieval-span "vector-search"
  (fn () (search index query))
  {:top-k 5})
```

See the [Observability guide](/docs/llm/observability).

entries.name: otel/set-attribute
entries.summary: Set one attribute on the innermost active span (the current `otel/span`, `agent/run`, or `llm/*` span). The key is a keyword or string; the value keeps its type — integers, floats, and booleans render as numbers/bools in the backend, not strings. A no-op when telemetry is disabled or there is no active span.
entries.body:
Set one attribute on the innermost active span (the current `otel/span`, `agent/run`, or `llm/*` span). The key is a keyword or string; the value keeps its type — integers, floats, and booleans render as numbers/bools in the backend, not strings. A no-op when telemetry is disabled or there is no active span.

**Signature:** `(otel/set-attribute key value) → nil`

```sema
(with-span "fetch" {}
  (otel/set-attribute :http.status 200)
  (otel/set-attribute :cache.hit true))
```

See the [Observability guide](/docs/llm/observability).

entries.name: otel/set-attributes
entries.summary: Set many attributes on the innermost active span from a map. Keys are keywords or strings; values keep their type (number/bool/string). Equivalent to calling `otel/set-attribute` for each entry. A no-op when telemetry is disabled or there is no active span.
entries.body:
Set many attributes on the innermost active span from a map. Keys are keywords or strings; values keep their type (number/bool/string). Equivalent to calling `otel/set-attribute` for each entry. A no-op when telemetry is disabled or there is no active span.

**Signature:** `(otel/set-attributes attrs-map) → nil`

```sema
(with-span "query" {}
  (otel/set-attributes {:rows 42 :cache.hit true :table "users"}))
```

See the [Observability guide](/docs/llm/observability).

entries.name: otel/set-status
entries.summary: Set the status of the innermost active span. `:ok` marks success; `:error` marks failure and records an `error.type` attribute plus the optional message. A no-op when telemetry is disabled or there is no active span. (Span-wrapping forms like `otel/span` already set Error status automatically when their body throws — use this for finer control.)
entries.body:
Set the status of the innermost active span. `:ok` marks success; `:error` marks failure and records an `error.type` attribute plus the optional message. A no-op when telemetry is disabled or there is no active span. (Span-wrapping forms like `otel/span` already set Error status automatically when their body throws — use this for finer control.)

**Signature:** `(otel/set-status :ok) → nil` · `(otel/set-status :error msg) → nil`

```sema
(with-span "upstream" {}
  (if (ok? (call-api))
      (otel/set-status :ok)
      (otel/set-status :error "upstream timeout")))
```

See the [Observability guide](/docs/llm/observability).

entries.name: otel/span
entries.summary: Run a thunk inside a named OpenTelemetry INTERNAL span and return the thunk's value. The span ends (recording its duration) when the thunk returns, and is marked with Error status if the thunk throws. An optional attributes map is attached to the span. Any LLM/tool spans created during the thunk nest beneath it. A no-op when telemetry is disabled. The `with-span` macro is the ergonomic form: `(with-span name attrs body…)`.
entries.body:
Run a thunk inside a named OpenTelemetry INTERNAL span and return the thunk's value. The span ends (recording its duration) when the thunk returns, and is marked with Error status if the thunk throws. An optional attributes map is attached to the span. Any LLM/tool spans created during the thunk nest beneath it. A no-op when telemetry is disabled. The `with-span` macro is the ergonomic form: `(with-span name attrs body…)`.

**Signature:** `(otel/span name thunk) → any` · `(otel/span name thunk attrs) → any`

```sema
(otel/span "ingest-batch"
  (fn ()
    (otel/event "started" {:batch-size 100})
    (+ 40 2))
  {:batch.size 100})
; => 42  (and emits one INTERNAL span named "ingest-batch")
```

Telemetry is opt-in: spans are only exported when an OTLP endpoint or `SEMA_OTEL_FILE` is configured. See the [Observability guide](/docs/llm/observability).

entries.name: otel/tool-span
entries.summary: Run a thunk inside a typed **TOOL** span for a tool you invoke yourself. Sets `gen_ai.operation.name` = `execute_tool`, `gen_ai.tool.name` = `name`, and (when `SEMA_OTEL_COMPAT` is set) the backend-native TOOL span-kind, so it classifies like the built-in tool spans. Optional third arg is an attributes map. A no-op when telemetry is disabled.
entries.body:
Run a thunk inside a typed **TOOL** span for a tool you invoke yourself. Sets `gen_ai.operation.name` = `execute_tool`, `gen_ai.tool.name` = `name`, and (when `SEMA_OTEL_COMPAT` is set) the backend-native TOOL span-kind, so it classifies like the built-in tool spans. Optional third arg is an attributes map. A no-op when telemetry is disabled.

**Signature:** `(otel/tool-span name thunk) → any` · `(otel/tool-span name thunk attrs) → any`

```sema
(otel/tool-span "lookup-weather"
  (fn () (weather "Oslo"))
  {:call-id "c-1"})
```

See the [Observability guide](/docs/llm/observability).

entries.name: otel/with-session
entries.summary: Run a thunk with a session scope: every span started inside (including `llm/*` and `agent/*` calls) is tagged with the session id, grouping the run in session-aware backends (Langfuse Sessions/Users). The optional config map carries `:user`. The previous scope is restored when the thunk returns. A no-op when telemetry is disabled. The `with-session` macro is the ergonomic form.
examples:
(otel/with-session "chat-42" {:user "alice"}
  (fn ()
    (llm/complete "...")          ; grouped under session chat-42, user alice
    (my-custom-pipeline)))
entries.body:
Run a thunk with a session scope: every span started inside (including `llm/*` and `agent/*` calls) is tagged with the session id, grouping the run in session-aware backends (Langfuse Sessions/Users). The optional config map carries `:user`. The previous scope is restored when the thunk returns. A no-op when telemetry is disabled. The `with-session` macro is the ergonomic form.

**Signature:** `(otel/with-session id thunk) → any` · `(otel/with-session id config thunk) → any`

```sema
(otel/with-session "chat-42" {:user "alice"}
  (fn ()
    (llm/complete "...")          ; grouped under session chat-42, user alice
    (my-custom-pipeline)))
```

See the [Observability guide](/docs/llm/observability).

entries.name: with-session
entries.summary: Macro: run the body with a session scope so every span started inside (including `llm/*` and `agent/*` calls) is grouped under the session id in session-aware backends (Langfuse Sessions/Users). The config map carries an optional `:user`; use `{}` for none. The ergonomic form of `(otel/with-session id config thunk)`. A no-op when telemetry is disabled.
entries.body:
Macro: run the body with a session scope so every span started inside (including `llm/*` and `agent/*` calls) is grouped under the session id in session-aware backends (Langfuse Sessions/Users). The config map carries an optional `:user`; use `{}` for none. The ergonomic form of `(otel/with-session id config thunk)`. A no-op when telemetry is disabled.

```sema
(with-session "chat-42" {:user "alice"}
  (llm/complete "...")          ; grouped under session chat-42, user alice
  (my-custom-pipeline))
```

See the [Observability guide](/docs/llm/observability).

entries.name: with-span
entries.summary: Macro: run the body inside a named OpenTelemetry span carrying an attributes map, and return the body's value. The span ends on exit (with Error status if the body throws). The ergonomic form of `(otel/span name thunk attrs)`. Use `{}` for no attributes. A no-op when telemetry is disabled.
examples:
(with-span "ingest-batch" {:batch.size 100}
  (otel/set-attribute :rows 42)
  (process-batch))
;; emits one INTERNAL span "ingest-batch"; nested llm/tool spans sit beneath it
entries.body:
Macro: run the body inside a named OpenTelemetry span carrying an attributes map, and return the body's value. The span ends on exit (with Error status if the body throws). The ergonomic form of `(otel/span name thunk attrs)`. Use `{}` for no attributes. A no-op when telemetry is disabled.

```sema
(with-span "ingest-batch" {:batch.size 100}
  (otel/set-attribute :rows 42)
  (process-batch))
;; emits one INTERNAL span "ingest-batch"; nested llm/tool spans sit beneath it
```

See the [Observability guide](/docs/llm/observability).

entries.name: pdf/extract-text
entries.summary: Extract all text from a PDF file, concatenated across all pages.
examples:
(pdf/extract-text "invoice.pdf")
; => "Invoice\nDate: 2025-01-15\nAmount: $50.00 USD\n..."

;; Clean up whitespace for LLM processing
(text/clean-whitespace (pdf/extract-text "invoice.pdf"))
; => "Invoice Date: 2025-01-15 Amount: $50.00 USD ..."
entries.body:
Extract all text from a PDF file, concatenated across all pages.

```sema
(pdf/extract-text "invoice.pdf")
; => "Invoice\nDate: 2025-01-15\nAmount: $50.00 USD\n..."

;; Clean up whitespace for LLM processing
(text/clean-whitespace (pdf/extract-text "invoice.pdf"))
; => "Invoice Date: 2025-01-15 Amount: $50.00 USD ..."
```

entries.name: pdf/extract-text-pages
entries.summary: Extract text from a PDF, returning a list of strings — one per page.
examples:
(pdf/extract-text-pages "report.pdf")
; => ("Page 1 content..." "Page 2 content..." "Page 3 content...")

;; Get text from a specific page
(nth (pdf/extract-text-pages "report.pdf") 0)
; => "Page 1 content..."

;; Process each page separately
(for-each
  (fn (page-text)
    (println (format "Page has ~a words" (text/word-count page-text))))
  (pdf/extract-text-pages "report.pdf"))
entries.body:
Extract text from a PDF, returning a list of strings — one per page.

```sema
(pdf/extract-text-pages "report.pdf")
; => ("Page 1 content..." "Page 2 content..." "Page 3 content...")

;; Get text from a specific page
(nth (pdf/extract-text-pages "report.pdf") 0)
; => "Page 1 content..."

;; Process each page separately
(for-each
  (fn (page-text)
    (println (format "Page has ~a words" (text/word-count page-text))))
  (pdf/extract-text-pages "report.pdf"))
```

entries.name: pdf/metadata
entries.summary: Return a map of PDF metadata fields. Always includes `:pages`; other fields (`:title`, `:author`, `:subject`, `:creator`, `:producer`) are included when present in the PDF.
examples:
(pprint (pdf/metadata "document.pdf"))
; => {:author "John Doe"
;     :creator "LibreOffice Writer"
;     :pages 5
;     :producer "LibreOffice"
;     :title "Quarterly Report"}

;; Access individual fields
(get (pdf/metadata "document.pdf") :title)
; => "Quarterly Report"

(get (pdf/metadata "document.pdf") :pages)
; => 5
entries.body:

```sema
;; Access individual fields
```

```sema
; => 12
```

entries.name: pio/assemble
entries.summary: Assemble a list of PIO instruction maps (interspersed with label symbols and the `:wrap-target` / `:wrap` keyword markers) into a binary program. Resolves labels, enforces the 32-instruction limit, and returns a map with `:instructions` (a little-endian bytevector of 16-bit words), `:length`, `:wrap-target`, and `:wrap`. The optional `config` map accepts `:side-set-bits` (0..5) and `:side-set-opt` (bool), which reserve delay/side-set bits accordingly.
entries.body:
Assemble a list of PIO instruction maps (interspersed with label symbols and the `:wrap-target` / `:wrap` keyword markers) into a binary program. Resolves labels, enforces the 32-instruction limit, and returns a map with `:instructions` (a little-endian bytevector of 16-bit words), `:length`, `:wrap-target`, and `:wrap`. The optional `config` map accepts `:side-set-bits` (0..5) and `:side-set-opt` (bool), which reserve delay/side-set bits accordingly.

```sema
(pio/assemble
  (list 'loop
        (pio/set :pins 1)
        (pio/set :pins 0)
        (pio/jmp :always 'loop))
  {:side-set-bits 0})
```

entries.name: pio/delay
entries.summary: Attach a post-instruction delay of `cycles` (0..31) to an existing PIO instruction map, stalling the state machine for that many cycles after the instruction runs. Returns a copy of `instr` with the delay field added. The available delay range shrinks as more side-set bits are configured.
entries.body:
Attach a post-instruction delay of `cycles` (0..31) to an existing PIO instruction map, stalling the state machine for that many cycles after the instruction runs. Returns a copy of `instr` with the delay field added. The available delay range shrinks as more side-set bits are configured.

```sema
(pio/delay 5 (pio/set :pins 1))
```

entries.name: pio/in
entries.summary: Build an RP2040 PIO `in` instruction that shifts `bits` (1..32) from `source` into the ISR. Valid sources: `:pins`, `:x`, `:y`, `:null`, `:isr`, `:osr`.
examples: (pio/in :pins 8)  ; shift 8 bits from the input pins into the ISR
entries.body:
Build an RP2040 PIO `in` instruction that shifts `bits` (1..32) from `source` into the ISR. Valid sources: `:pins`, `:x`, `:y`, `:null`, `:isr`, `:osr`.

```sema
(pio/in :pins 8)  ; shift 8 bits from the input pins into the ISR
```

entries.name: pio/irq
entries.summary: Build an RP2040 PIO `irq` instruction that operates on IRQ flag `index` (0..7). `mode` is one of `:set` (raise the flag), `:wait` (raise it and stall until cleared), or `:clear` (lower it). Pass an optional trailing `:rel` keyword to make the index relative to the state machine.
entries.body:
Build an RP2040 PIO `irq` instruction that operates on IRQ flag `index` (0..7). `mode` is one of `:set` (raise the flag), `:wait` (raise it and stall until cleared), or `:clear` (lower it). Pass an optional trailing `:rel` keyword to make the index relative to the state machine.

```sema
(pio/irq :set 0)
(pio/irq :wait 1 :rel)
```

entries.name: pio/jmp
entries.summary: Build an RP2040 PIO `jmp` instruction. Called with a single `target` symbol it is an unconditional jump (`:always`); called with `(pio/jmp cond target)` it jumps only when the condition holds. Valid conditions: `:always`, `:!x`, `:x--`, `:!y`, `:y--`, `:x!=y`, `:pin`, `:!osre`. `target` is a label symbol resolved at assembly time.
entries.body:
Build an RP2040 PIO `jmp` instruction. Called with a single `target` symbol it is an unconditional jump (`:always`); called with `(pio/jmp cond target)` it jumps only when the condition holds. Valid conditions: `:always`, `:!x`, `:x--`, `:!y`, `:y--`, `:x!=y`, `:pin`, `:!osre`. `target` is a label symbol resolved at assembly time.

```sema
(pio/jmp :x-- 'loop)
```

entries.name: pio/mov
entries.summary: Build an RP2040 PIO `mov` instruction copying `source` to `dest`. Valid destinations: `:pins`, `:x`, `:y`, `:exec`, `:pc`. Valid sources: `:pins`, `:x`, `:y`, `:null`, `:status`, `:isr`, `:osr`. An optional third `op` keyword applies a transform: `:invert` (bitwise NOT) or `:reverse` (bit-reverse).
examples:
(pio/mov :x :osr)            ; copy OSR into X
(pio/mov :y :x :invert)      ; copy ~X into Y
entries.body:
Build an RP2040 PIO `mov` instruction copying `source` to `dest`. Valid destinations: `:pins`, `:x`, `:y`, `:exec`, `:pc`. Valid sources: `:pins`, `:x`, `:y`, `:null`, `:status`, `:isr`, `:osr`. An optional third `op` keyword applies a transform: `:invert` (bitwise NOT) or `:reverse` (bit-reverse).

```sema
(pio/mov :x :osr)            ; copy OSR into X
(pio/mov :y :x :invert)      ; copy ~X into Y
```

entries.name: pio/nop
entries.summary: Build an RP2040 PIO `nop` instruction (encoded as `mov y, y`, which has no side effects). Returns an instruction map for use in a program passed to `pio/assemble`.
entries.body:
Build an RP2040 PIO `nop` instruction (encoded as `mov y, y`, which has no side effects). Returns an instruction map for use in a program passed to `pio/assemble`.

```sema
(pio/nop)  ; => an instruction map
```

entries.name: pio/out
entries.summary: Build an RP2040 PIO `out` instruction that shifts `bits` (1..32) out of the OSR into `dest`. Valid destinations: `:pins`, `:x`, `:y`, `:null`, `:pindirs`, `:pc`, `:isr`, `:exec`.
examples: (pio/out :pins 1)  ; shift 1 bit from the OSR onto the output pins
entries.body:
Build an RP2040 PIO `out` instruction that shifts `bits` (1..32) out of the OSR into `dest`. Valid destinations: `:pins`, `:x`, `:y`, `:null`, `:pindirs`, `:pc`, `:isr`, `:exec`.

```sema
(pio/out :pins 1)  ; shift 1 bit from the OSR onto the output pins
```

entries.name: pio/pull
entries.summary: Build an RP2040 PIO `pull` instruction that moves a word from the TX FIFO into the OSR. Accepts up to two option keywords: `:block` (default) / `:no-block` controls whether to stall when the FIFO is empty, and `:ifempty` only pulls once the OSR has reached its shift threshold.
entries.body:
Build an RP2040 PIO `pull` instruction that moves a word from the TX FIFO into the OSR. Accepts up to two option keywords: `:block` (default) / `:no-block` controls whether to stall when the FIFO is empty, and `:ifempty` only pulls once the OSR has reached its shift threshold.

```sema
(pio/pull :ifempty :block)
```

entries.name: pio/push
entries.summary: Build an RP2040 PIO `push` instruction that moves the ISR contents into the RX FIFO. Accepts up to two option keywords: `:block` (default) / `:no-block` controls whether to stall when the FIFO is full, and `:iffull` only pushes once the ISR has reached its shift threshold.
entries.body:
Build an RP2040 PIO `push` instruction that moves the ISR contents into the RX FIFO. Accepts up to two option keywords: `:block` (default) / `:no-block` controls whether to stall when the FIFO is full, and `:iffull` only pushes once the ISR has reached its shift threshold.

```sema
(pio/push :iffull :block)
```

entries.name: pio/set
entries.summary: Build an RP2040 PIO `set` instruction that writes an immediate `value` (0..31) to `dest`. Valid destinations: `:pins`, `:x`, `:y`, `:pindirs`.
examples:
(pio/set :pins 1)   ; drive the set pins high
(pio/set :x 0)      ; load 0 into X
entries.body:
Build an RP2040 PIO `set` instruction that writes an immediate `value` (0..31) to `dest`. Valid destinations: `:pins`, `:x`, `:y`, `:pindirs`.

```sema
(pio/set :pins 1)   ; drive the set pins high
(pio/set :x 0)      ; load 0 into X
```

entries.name: pio/side
entries.summary: Attach a side-set `value` (0..31) to an existing PIO instruction map, setting the side-set pins in the same cycle the instruction executes. Returns a copy of `instr` with the side-set field added. The side-set width must be configured via the `:side-set-bits` option to `pio/assemble`.
examples: (pio/side 1 (pio/nop))   ; nop while driving side-set pins to 1
entries.body:
Attach a side-set `value` (0..31) to an existing PIO instruction map, setting the side-set pins in the same cycle the instruction executes. Returns a copy of `instr` with the side-set field added. The side-set width must be configured via the `:side-set-bits` option to `pio/assemble`.

```sema
(pio/side 1 (pio/nop))   ; nop while driving side-set pins to 1
```

entries.name: pio/wait
entries.summary: Build an RP2040 PIO `wait` instruction that stalls until `source` at `index` matches `polarity` (0 or 1). Valid sources: `:gpio`, `:pin`, `:irq`. `index` is in the range 0..31. Pass an optional trailing `:rel` keyword to make an IRQ index relative to the state machine.
examples:
(pio/wait 1 :pin 0)         ; wait for pin 0 to go high
(pio/wait 1 :irq 0 :rel)    ; wait for relative IRQ 0
entries.body:
Build an RP2040 PIO `wait` instruction that stalls until `source` at `index` matches `polarity` (0 or 1). Valid sources: `:gpio`, `:pin`, `:irq`. `index` is in the range 0..31. Pass an optional trailing `:rel` keyword to make an IRQ index relative to the state machine.

```sema
(pio/wait 1 :pin 0)         ; wait for pin 0 to go high
(pio/wait 1 :irq 0 :rel)    ; wait for relative IRQ 0
```

entries.name: web/user-agent
entries.summary: Return the browser's `navigator.userAgent` string. Works in all browsers.

Return the browser's `navigator.userAgent` string. Works in all browsers.

entries.name: web/user-agent-data
entries.summary: Return structured browser information from `navigator.userAgentData`. Returns a map on Chromium-based browsers (Chrome, Edge, Opera), `nil` on Firefox and Safari.
entries.body:
Return structured browser information from `navigator.userAgentData`. Returns a map on Chromium-based browsers (Chrome, Edge, Opera), `nil` on Firefox and Safari.

```sema
(web/user-agent-data)
; Chromium => {:mobile false :platform "macOS" :brands ("Chromium/120" "Google Chrome/120")}
; Firefox/Safari => nil
```

`userAgentData` is the modern replacement for UA string parsing — it returns structured, reliable data instead of a messy string. However, it's Chromium-only. Use `web/user-agent` for cross-browser compatibility.

entries.summary: Equality. For numbers this is numeric equality (so `(= 1 1.0)` is `#t`); for non-numbers it falls back to structural equality. Unlike `<` / `>`, comparing non-numbers does not error — it reports whether the values are equal.
entries.body:
Equality. For numbers this is numeric equality (so `(= 1 1.0)` is `#t`); for
non-numbers it falls back to structural equality. Unlike `<` / `>`, comparing
non-numbers does not error — it reports whether the values are equal.

```sema
(= 1 1)           ; => #t
(= 1 1.0)         ; => #t
(= 1 2)           ; => #f
(= "abc" "abc")   ; => #t   (structural, not an error)
(= 1 "a")         ; => #f
```

entries.name: define-record-type
entries.summary: Define a new record type, generating a constructor, predicate, and one accessor per field.
examples:
(define-record-type point
  (make-point x y)       ; constructor (positional args)
  point?                  ; predicate
  (x point-x)            ; (field-name accessor-name)
  (y point-y))
entries.body:
Define a new record type, generating a constructor, predicate, and one accessor per field.

```sema
(define-record-type point
  (make-point x y)       ; constructor (positional args)
  point?                  ; predicate
  (x point-x)            ; (field-name accessor-name)
  (y point-y))
```

General syntax:

```sema
(define-record-type <type-name>
  (<constructor> <field-name> ...)
  <predicate>
  (<field-name> <accessor>) ...)
```

entries.name: record?
entries.summary: Test if a value is any record instance (of any record type).
entries.body:
Test if a value is any record instance (of any record type).

```sema
(record? (make-point 3 4))   ; => #t
(record? {:x 3 :y 4})        ; => #f
(record? 42)                 ; => #f
```

entries.name: type
entries.summary: Return the type of a value as a keyword. For records, returns the record's type name:
entries.body:
Return the type of a value as a keyword. For records, returns the record's type name:

```sema
(type (make-point 3 4))   ; => :point
(type [1 2 3])            ; => :vector
(type {:a 1})             ; => :map
```

Inside `#"..."`, use `\"`:

```sema
(regex/match? #"\"[^\"]+\"" "say \"hello\"")
; => #t
```

entries.name: regex/find-all
entries.summary: Find all non-overlapping matches of a pattern.
entries.body:
Find all non-overlapping matches of a pattern.

```sema
(regex/find-all #"\d+" "a1b2c3")          ; => ("1" "2" "3")
(regex/find-all #"[A-Z]" "Hello World")   ; => ("H" "W")
```

entries.name: regex/match
entries.summary: Match a pattern and return match details as a map, or `nil` if no match.
entries.body:
Match a pattern and return match details as a map, or `nil` if no match.

**Signature:** `(regex/match pattern text) → map | nil`

The returned map contains:

| Key | Value |
|-----|-------|
| `:match` | The full matched substring |
| `:groups` | List of capture groups (group 1, 2, …) |
| `:start` | Start byte offset in the input |
| `:end` | End byte offset in the input |

```sema
(regex/match #"(\d+)-(\w+)" "item-42-foo")
; => {:match "42-foo" :groups ("42" "foo") :start 5 :end 11}

(regex/match #"xyz" "abc")
; => nil
```

Optional capture groups that don't participate in the match become `nil`:

```sema
(regex/match #"(\d+)(?:-(\d+))?" "42")
; => {:match "42" :groups ("42" nil) :start 0 :end 2}
```

`:start` and `:end` are byte offsets (UTF-8). For ASCII text they match character indices, but for non-ASCII they may differ.

entries.name: regex/match?
entries.summary: Test if a pattern matches anywhere in a string. Returns `#t` or `#f`.
entries.body:
Test if a pattern matches anywhere in a string. Returns `#t` or `#f`.

```sema
(regex/match? #"\d+" "abc123")       ; => #t
(regex/match? #"\d+" "no digits")    ; => #f
(regex/match? #"^\d+$" "abc123")     ; => #f  (anchored — must match entire string)
(regex/match? #"^\d+$" "123")        ; => #t
```

entries.name: regex/replace
entries.summary: Replace the **first** match of a pattern.
entries.body:
Replace the **first** match of a pattern.

**Signature:** `(regex/replace pattern replacement text) → string`

```sema
(regex/replace #"\d+" "X" "a1b2c3")    ; => "aXb2c3"
```
